agent_api_gateway.proof_boundary: The gateway executes local product operations with external effects disabled; it does not expose a live public API, credentials, payment integration, or claim-opening authority.

audit_rules: Record actor, organization, packet_id, tool, scope, confirmation state, dry_run flag, and external_effects_allowed for every agent tool call.
Record billing estimate and authorization result before heavy or metered jobs run.
Record blocked forbidden-tool attempts as audit events with can_open_claim_gate=false.
confirmation_rules: Customer-visible packet creation, report generation, safe-summary generation, broker packet generation, and billing quotes require explicit user confirmation.
External sends are always disabled even after confirmation.
Confirmation can start local work only; it cannot open customs, tariff, legal, buyer, supplier, payment, or shipment claims.
agent_api_manifest.proof_boundary: This is a local API/MCP contract. It does not expose a live public API gateway or payment integration.
safety_rules: Agents can create packets and reports only through backend claim/blocker rules.
Agents cannot approve import/export, tariff, supplier, buyer, shipment, or launch claims.
Billable or heavy jobs require plan/credit authorization before execution.
Agents must preserve audit, confirmation, and scope rules for every tool call.
scope_rules: Every tool must execute inside a packet or read-only coverage scope.
Write tools require packet:write scope and cannot modify external systems.
Read tools require packet:read scope and must not expose private uploaded file contents outside the local product.
Billing-sensitive tools require plan and credit authorization before worker execution.

all_stage_readiness.proof_boundary: Stage 0 and Stages 1-18 are represented by local routes, APIs, generated artifacts, tests, and blocker rows. Real reviewers, private-beta users, production hosting, monitoring, legal/privacy/security approval, payment activation, qualified trade review, and public go-live owner approval remain external gates.

stages.acceptance: No customer-facing route or report may imply approval, tariff confirmation, CFIA clearance, supplier verification, buyer validation, or legal/customs advice.
stages.external_gate: Qualified legal/customs/privacy review is still required before public claims or advice language changes.
stages.local_proof: Claim-boundary copy, blocked-claims manifests, product check, and package audit all preserve the promise: Before you import or export, know what is missing.
stages.name: Freeze product promise
stages.next_valid_move: Keep the promise frozen and route any stronger claim to qualified review plus dated evidence.

stages.acceptance: A beginner can start with unknowns and leave with a starter checklist and blockers.
stages.external_gate: Real beginner usability still needs private-beta users.
stages.local_proof: Starter packet generation preserves unknown answers as missing-evidence blockers.
stages.name: Beginner Start UX
stages.next_valid_move: Run timed beginner smoke tests with real users and record misunderstandings.

stages.acceptance: Digital-text PDFs produce extracted fields with confidence; scanned PDFs create OCR_REQUIRED blockers.
stages.external_gate: Hosted upload security and OCR cost controls need qualified security/privacy review before public use.
stages.local_proof: Document triage, file limits, native extraction, OCR_REQUIRED routing, and confirmation gates are tested locally.
stages.name: Real PDF quick check
stages.next_valid_move: Run public-upload security review and hosted sandbox proof before opening production uploads.

stages.acceptance: Critical product, country, buyer, supplier, and HS-code fields remain blocked until confirmed or corrected.
stages.external_gate: Customer acceptance needs real user smoke testing.
stages.local_proof: Confirmation routes, grouped blockers, evidence ledger, and draft-unconfirmed behavior are exercised in local tests.
stages.name: Field confirmation and missing evidence
stages.next_valid_move: Observe users correcting extracted fields and record any failed comprehension as blocker rows.

stages.acceptance: Reports include summary, trade direction, documents, missing evidence, blocked claims, next valid moves, questions, coverage, AI/no-AI disclosure, and not-advice boundaries.
stages.external_gate: Report-language reviewer must approve customer-facing language before public launch.
stages.local_proof: Starter, missing-evidence, buyer, broker, expert, and ChatGPT-safe report artifacts are generated with closed claims.
stages.name: Customer-grade reports
stages.next_valid_move: Send generated report samples to product, trade-boundary, and report-language reviewers.

stages.acceptance: Uploaded files are quarantined, generated-named, signature checked, size/page limited, not directly served, audited, deletable, and retention-gated.
stages.external_gate: Production malware scanning, rate-limit enforcement, and public upload sandbox need security signoff.
stages.local_proof: Local upload routes enforce PDF limits, no direct serving, delete-files behavior, audit rows, parser limits, rate-limit policy, and malware-scan compensating control.
stages.name: Public upload hardening
stages.next_valid_move: Run hosted upload threat review and decide malware scanning provider or compensating control owner.

stages.acceptance: No customer document reaches AI unless organization policy and document permission allow it; AI can create blockers only.
stages.external_gate: Live AI providers remain disabled until privacy/security/legal review and explicit configuration.
stages.local_proof: AI validation rejects gate-opening output, unsafe claim language, missing evidence IDs, and cross-organization evidence references.
stages.name: AI/no-AI production controls
stages.next_valid_move: Run prompt-injection/security review against hosted parser and model-route configuration.

stages.acceptance: A user can create/save a packet locally, return through the workspace, inspect history, request deletion, manage AI policy, and use role-gated views.
stages.external_gate: Hosted identity, account lifecycle, privacy, and deletion process require review before real users.
stages.local_proof: Local session auth, seeded users/orgs, RBAC, packet history, audit view, report history, and deletion requests are generated and tested.
stages.name: Saved workspace and account path
stages.next_valid_move: Assign identity/privacy owner and run hosted account/session smoke before private beta.

stages.acceptance: Role-specific review packets, scoped review requests, finding ingestion, and blocker conversion exist locally.
stages.external_gate: Real qualified reviewer findings must exist before any public launch or advice claim.
stages.local_proof: The product generates expert queues and scoped review packets while preserving human approval gates.
stages.name: External expert review stage
stages.next_valid_move: Freeze the package and collect scoped findings from security, privacy/legal, AI safety, UX, trade-boundary, logistics, report-language, billing, and ops reviewers.

stages.acceptance: Monitored source changes can mark packets stale and create review-required alerts.
stages.external_gate: Live official-source fetching needs terms/robots/API permission and current-source owner review.
stages.local_proof: Source registry, snapshots, hashes, diff classifications, packet impacts, stale blockers, and SQLite store are generated locally.
stages.name: Policy/source monitoring
stages.next_valid_move: Approve a source-refresh policy and run dated official-source refresh proof.

stages.acceptance: Every country-pair result shows coverage level and blocks unsupported country-specific claims.
stages.external_gate: Country-specific copy above generic readiness needs current sources and qualified review.
stages.local_proof: Tier 0-5 coverage policy, country pair rows, and Tier 5 claim gate are generated and tested.
stages.name: Country coverage logic
stages.next_valid_move: Promote a country/category only after monitored official sources and expert-reviewed templates exist.

stages.acceptance: Opportunity rows show signals, provenance, confidence, buyer-validation-missing state, and create-packet hints without recommendations.
stages.external_gate: Demand, margin, supplier, and buyer validation remain external evidence lanes.
stages.local_proof: Signal rows, source provenance, confidence fields, and packet-creation hints are generated with demand/profit/buyer claims blocked.
stages.name: Opportunity scanner
stages.next_valid_move: Attach dated market/source/user evidence before treating a signal as an opportunity decision.

stages.acceptance: The product generates freight-forwarder questions and missing transport evidence without guaranteeing route, cost, or delivery.
stages.external_gate: Freight-forwarder selection, route, cost, insurance, and delivery claims require qualified/logistics evidence.
stages.local_proof: Incoterms, mode, weight/dimensions, packing list, commercial invoice, cold-chain, dangerous goods, insurance, and forwarder checks are generated.
stages.name: Transport readiness lane
stages.next_valid_move: Send the transport packet to a freight forwarder and record scoped dated feedback.

stages.acceptance: Heavy actions are blocked behind local credits/subscription/confirmation; live checkout stays disabled until pricing is validated.
stages.external_gate: Live checkout, invoices, refunds, tax, pricing, and payment capture require finance/legal/payment-provider approval.
stages.local_proof: OCR, AI, large packets, premium reports, monitoring, agent/API, and expert-review metering rows exist with no live charges.
stages.name: Billing and credits
stages.next_valid_move: Validate pricing and payment-provider terms before enabling checkout.

stages.acceptance: Agent calls are scoped, audited, metered, and cannot create external effects without user confirmation.
stages.external_gate: Public API gateway, keys, quotas, third-party integrations, and payment routes remain disabled.
stages.local_proof: Allowed and forbidden tools, audit rules, scope rules, confirmation rules, and local executor proof are generated.
stages.name: Agent/API layer
stages.next_valid_move: Run API security review and issue scoped keys only after product-owner approval.

stages.acceptance: Normal users can understand the flow, see plain-English blockers and next actions, and do not misread output as approval.
stages.external_gate: Five-user usability test remains required before public launch.
stages.local_proof: Local UI audit covers desktop/mobile H1s, labels, overflow, blank controls, stale copy, tracebacks, and local-path leaks.
stages.name: UX and usability
stages.next_valid_move: Run private-beta usability sessions for beginner, document, report, save, and deletion flows.

stages.acceptance: Private beta can run only after named owners exist for operations, security, support, rollback, incident response, backups, monitoring, and privacy/terms.
stages.external_gate: Real staging/production hosting, TLS, secrets, backups, monitoring, support, and incident response require operator/security signoff.
stages.local_proof: Docker/Compose, env example, health endpoints, deployment report, private-beta checklist, and CI proof exist locally.
stages.name: Production deployment readiness
stages.next_valid_move: Provision staging and assign named owners for hosting, security, support, rollback, backups, and incident response.

stages.acceptance: The product has controlled-beta smoke scenarios and owner gates, but real users must complete the flows before beta approval.
stages.external_gate: Minimum beta users and reviewer/broker/freight-forwarder feedback are not simulated as proof.
stages.local_proof: Private-beta checklist, launch controls, team workspace, support/feedback path, and closed public-launch state are generated.
stages.name: Private beta
stages.next_valid_move: Recruit beta users, run the listed smoke tests, record outcomes, and fix critical findings.

stages.acceptance: The safe public subset is defined and measured locally; public launch stays blocked until costs, support, hosting, reviews, beta outcomes, and owner approval pass.
stages.external_gate: Public launch approval, production deployment, legal/privacy/security signoff, monitoring, support, and real-user smoke success are required.
stages.local_proof: Beginner starter, PDF quick check, starter/missing-evidence reports, account save, AI/privacy settings, no live approvals, no unrestricted OCR/AI, and no autonomous effects are all represented locally.
stages.name: Public go-live
stages.next_valid_move: After private-beta fixes, run go/no-go and explicitly approve the safe public subset.

authorization_algorithm: require confirmation for OCR batches, AI jobs, source monitoring, and agent/API execution
refund or adjust on failed worker job

metering_dimensions.heavy_job_threshold: large or batch OCR requires upgrade/manual approval
all model extraction, private AI, and deep-research jobs require authorization
agent tool execution requires scope and credit checks

billing_credit_controls.proof_boundary: Billing controls define metering and cost gates only; no live payment, invoice, or checkout session is created.

billing_usage_ledger.proof_boundary: The usage ledger estimates and blocks cost exposure locally; no payment, invoice, or live checkout is performed.

countries.next_valid_move: Add current monitored official sources, category templates, and qualified review before country-specific claims.

packet_coverage.next_valid_move: Use generic readiness output unless both countries and category have monitored/expert-reviewed coverage.

country_coverage.proof_boundary: Coverage tiers describe product support level only; they do not prove country-specific legal, customs, tariff, or compliance correctness.

tier_definitions.label: selectable only
tier_definitions.support: Country can be selected in generic product flows only.

tier_definitions.label: generic starter checklist
tier_definitions.minimum_evidence: Country appears in a packet but lacks monitored official sources.
tier_definitions.support: Generic import/export checklist language may be shown.

tier_definitions.label: reference sources catalogued
tier_definitions.minimum_evidence: At least one official or official-adjacent source is catalogued.
tier_definitions.support: Reference sources can orient research, with claim gates closed.

tier_definitions.label: monitored sources
tier_definitions.minimum_evidence: Multiple official sources are recorded with access dates.
tier_definitions.support: Multiple official sources are tracked for internal review prompts.

tier_definitions.label: category template support
tier_definitions.minimum_evidence: Country, category, and route templates are maintained.
tier_definitions.support: Category-specific templates can be prepared for expert review.

tier_definitions.label: expert-reviewed templates
tier_definitions.minimum_evidence: Current official sources, category templates, and qualified review are attached.
tier_definitions.support: Expert-reviewed templates may support scoped country-specific product copy.

expert_roles.label: Food / Regulated Product Reviewer
expert_roles.scope: CFIA or regulated product requirement review where applicable

expert_roles.label: Freight Forwarder
expert_roles.scope: mode, route, packing, insurance, temperature, and shipment questions

expert_roles.label: Privacy / Security Reviewer
expert_roles.scope: uploaded customer evidence, AI processing, retention, and hosted controls

expert_roles.label: Finance / Operations Reviewer
expert_roles.scope: credits, pricing, manual approval, and cost exposure

expert_network.proof_boundary: The product can prepare and route expert packets locally; real qualified review remains an external evidence gate.

review_queue.next_valid_move: Send scoped packet to a qualified person and record dated finding before opening any external claim.

launch_operations.proof_boundary: Launch operations are locally specified for private-beta review; they do not approve production deployment or public launch.

research_execution_plan.proof_boundary: Research execution routes the work to model-prior, web, official-source, dataset, and expert/user evidence lanes; it does not itself prove the external facts.

dataset_or_api_needs: trade volume or importer/exporter dataset if opportunity ranking is needed
logistics cost dataset or forwarder quote if route comparison is needed

rows.next_valid_move: Collect dated official-source records, dataset references, buyer/operator notes, and qualified review findings before external claims.

official_source_search: regulated product source if category requires it

team_workspace.proof_boundary: Team workspace roles and approvals are local coordination surfaces; they do not prove real owner approval or external launch permission.

workspaces.next_valid_move: Assign owners, collect evidence, and keep the packet in private review until gates are approved.

questions: Which Incoterms version and named place apply?
Who is importer of record and who pays duty, freight, and insurance?
freight_forwarder_packet_sections.title: Incoterms and delivery responsibility

questions: Which mode is intended: ocean, air, road, rail, courier, or multimodal?
Which origin/destination ports or terminals are planned?
freight_forwarder_packet_sections.title: Mode, route, ports, and transit constraints

questions: What are gross weight, net weight, dimensions, carton count, pallet count, and packaging type?
Does any wood packaging require ISPM 15 review?
freight_forwarder_packet_sections.title: Weight, dimensions, packing, and palletization

questions: Does the commercial invoice include seller, buyer, description, HS code if known, value, currency, origin, and Incoterms?
Does the packing list reconcile to invoice quantities and shipment units?
freight_forwarder_packet_sections.title: Commercial invoice and packing list

questions: Is temperature control required and what range/data logger proof is needed?
Are dangerous goods, batteries, chemicals, or restricted handling declarations required?
freight_forwarder_packet_sections.title: Cold-chain, dangerous goods, and special handling

questions: Which documents and shipment facts should be sent to a forwarder for a quote?
What remains internal until customer confirmation or qualified review is attached?
freight_forwarder_packet_sections.title: Freight-forwarder quote packet

freight_forwarder_questions: What mode, route, ports, and transit constraints apply?
Are dimensions, gross weight, packaging, and palletization known?
Do Incoterms create buyer, seller, or DDP/importer responsibility?
Is temperature control, insurance, dangerous goods, or wood packaging review required?
rows.next_valid_move: Collect shipment details and route to freight forwarder/broker review before transport cost or route claims.
packing_and_invoice_sections: cartons, pallets, marks, and packaging type
country of origin and HS code if known
special_handling_sections: dangerous goods declaration or negative confirmation
